description.en: External trend, weak signal, or trigger event.
description.zh: 外部趋势、弱信号或触发事件。

description.en: What changes for the business model in this future.
description.zh: 这个未来会如何改变商业模式。

description.en: Action useful across more than one scenario.
description.zh: 在多个情景下都值得做的动作。

headline.en: Explore futures before locking strategy to one forecast
headline.zh: 在押注单一预测之前，先展开多个未来

whyOpen.en: Use this when the question depends on external uncertainty. Two critical uncertainties create four futures, then you identify moves that stay useful across them.
whyOpen.zh: 当问题高度依赖外部不确定性时用它：用两个关键不确定性生成四个未来，再找跨情景都有效的动作。

audience.en: Teams facing regulation, technology shifts, demand shocks, or market-structure uncertainty.
audience.zh: 适合面对监管、技术变化、需求冲击或市场结构不确定性的团队。

firstSteps.en: Choose uncertainties that are both high-impact and genuinely unresolved.
firstSteps.zh: 选择影响大、且确实未定的不确定性。

firstSteps.en: Name each future as a plausible world, not as a best/worst case.
firstSteps.zh: 把每个未来命名成可信世界，而不是简单的最好/最坏情况。

outcomes.en: Four plausible futures, early signals to monitor, robust moves, and bets that only make sense in specific scenarios.
outcomes.zh: 四个可信未来、需要监测的早期信号、稳健动作，以及只在特定情景下成立的押注。

commonMisreads.en: Scenarios are not predictions; they are rehearsal spaces for strategic choices.
commonMisreads.zh: 情景不是预测，而是战略选择的演练空间。

practicePrompts.en: Which move would still make sense in at least three of the four futures?
practicePrompts.zh: 哪一个动作在四个未来中至少三个都仍然值得做？
